title: AES Holds Anti-Fraud and Financial Security Conference in Ouagadougou
detail: The Alliance of Sahel States (AES) hosted a three-day ministerial conference in Ouagadougou (April 6–8) titled 'Combating fraud in a context of security threats: challenges, system resilience and prospects for action,' opened by Burkina Faso's Minister of Economy, Finance and Foresight. Five panel sessions addressed threat assessment, interagency law enforcement cooperation, cross-border criminal network disruption, financial system resilience, and regional security coordination. The conference reflects the AES's effort to build independent institutional capacity in customs, financial intelligence, and anti-fraud enforcement — critical infrastructure as all three AES states face Western sanctions and diminished access to multilateral financial institutions such as the IMF and World Bank. The AES anti-fraud architecture is also being tied to the new BCID-AES development bank (500 billion CFA francs capital) inaugurated in late 2025.

title: Russia–Burkina Faso Diplomatic Meeting in Moscow to Expand AES Cooperation
detail: Russia's Deputy Foreign Affairs Minister G.E. Borisenko received Burkina Faso's Ambassador Aristide Tapsoba in Moscow on April 7, 2026, with talks focused on expanding Russia's economic and security cooperation with the Alliance of Sahel States. The meeting is part of a sustained diplomatic track between the AES bloc and Russia following the expulsion of Western military missions from all three AES states. Burkina Faso currently holds the AES rotating chairmanship under Captain Traoré. Russia has supplied arms, Africa Corps security personnel, and diplomatic cover at the UN Security Council. A Russia-AES joint ministerial meeting is expected in 2026 to formalize enhanced cooperation agreements.
